creator: bsam@ipt.ru
text:
On Thu, 12 Mar 2009 00:28:45 +0100 barbara wrote:

> This is what I have (6.4-STABLE):

> %pkg_info -Ix gegl
> gegl-0.0.22_1       A graph based image processing framework

> %pkg_info -Lx gegl | grep gtk-doc.\*api
> /usr/local/share/gtk-doc/html/gegl/api.html

> %ll /usr/local/share/gtk-doc/html/gegl/api.html
> -rwxr-xr-x  1 root  wheel  145887 Mar 11 01:19 /usr/local/share/gtk-doc/html/gegl/api.html

> So no problem about that file here.

That meens only that this file *sometimes* may be installed. I.e.
autotools detects an existing software and installs the file.
I use ports-mgmt/tinderbox to test packages which emulates a clean
system. That file is not installed there -- it's a port bug. May
be a needed dependency is missing or something else. It's a
maintainer's job to do. I did my best. ;-)

Thanks for the feedback.


WBR
-- 
bsam
